# EU Day-Ahead Electricity Prices

> Free, public REST API and landing page for live day-ahead electricity
> prices across 9 European markets (NL, DE, BE, FR, AT, CH, DK, NO, SE).
> Responses are hourly (or quarter-hour) EUR/kWh prices with optional
> markup and VAT applied per request.

Site: https://dap.xadi.eu/
OpenAPI: https://dap.xadi.eu/openapi.yaml
Source: https://github.com/axtg/eu-dayahead
Contact: hello@xadi.nl
License: MIT (code) · Data attribution: ENTSOE Transparency Platform, stekker.app

## Two ways to use this

1. **Hosted** — open https://dap.xadi.eu/ for the interactive UI, or call the
   public API directly (e.g. `curl https://dap.xadi.eu/api/nl/today`). No
   account or API key required. Subject to the rate limit and usage terms below.
2. **Self-hosted** — clone https://github.com/axtg/eu-dayahead, run
   `npm install && npm start`, and point requests at your own instance. For
   production use, register at https://transparency.entsoe.eu/ and set
   `ENTSOE_API_KEY` in `.env`. Same UI, same API, your infrastructure.

## Data sources

- **Primary**: ENTSOE Transparency Platform (requires an API key when
  self-hosting). Quarter-hour resolution for most zones, hourly for
  Switzerland. The default `interval=60M` aggregates quarters to hourly means.
- **Fallback**: stekker.app — used only when ENTSOE is unavailable or no key
  is configured. Limited country coverage (rejects several non-NL bidding
  zones such as DE-LU) and a known artifact at HH:00 quarters. When Stekker
  serves the response, the body includes a top-level `warnings` array.

## Endpoints

- GET /api/countries — list supported countries with metadata
- GET /api/{country}/today — today's 24 hourly prices in the country's local timezone
- GET /api/{country}/next24h — the next 24 hours starting from the current hour
- GET /api/{country}/next/{hours} — the next N hours (1–48)
- GET /api/providers — list supported provider markup presets
- GET /api/providers/next-energy — Netherlands Next Energy preset
- GET /api/providers/{provider}/{country} — apply a provider preset to any country
- GET /health — service health
- GET /health/cache — upstream cache statistics
- GET /openapi.yaml — full OpenAPI 3 specification

## Path parameters

- {country} — ISO-2 lowercase: nl, de, be, fr, at, ch, dk, no, se
- {provider} — slug: next-energy, vattenfall, eneco

## Query parameters

- markup (decimal, EUR/kWh) — fixed per-kWh markup; alias: fixedMarkup
- variableMarkup (decimal) — proportional markup, e.g. 0.05 = +5%
- vat (decimal) — VAT factor, e.g. 0.21 for 21%; or "auto" to use country default
- autoVat (boolean) — true to apply the country's default VAT
- includeVat (boolean) — return VAT-inclusive prices
- roundTo (integer, default 5) — number of decimal places in returned prices
- interval (enum) — granularity of the returned series:
    - `60M` (default) — hourly mean of the underlying quarter-hour values
    - `15M` — pass-through quarter-hour values (~4× more entries per window)
  Invalid values return HTTP 400.

## Rate limits

- Per-IP token bucket: 10 requests per second (default)
- Headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, X-RateLimit-Reset
- 429 responses include Retry-After

## Caching

- Upstream raw responses are cached for 1 hour, keyed by country + window.
- Markup and VAT are applied per request after cache lookup, so changing
  these parameters never re-hits the upstream.

## Usage terms

- Free for personal, research, educational, and other non-commercial use.
- For commercial or high-volume use: contact sales@xadi.nl.
- When redistributing prices, attribute ENTSOE Transparency Platform and stekker.app.
